answer:
Authentic narrations explicitly identify associating partners with Allah (shirk) as the most severe of all major sins. When asked to identify the greatest and most grievous sin in the sight of Allah, the Prophet Muhammad clearly defined it as setting up a rival to Allah despite the fact that He alone created you [1-3]. 

This directly anchors the definition of major shirk by highlighting the ultimate injustice: attributing divine rights or equals to others when Allah is the sole Creator of humanity [1, 3]. 

Furthermore, the Prophet consistently placed shirk at the very top of explicitly defined categories of major offenses:
*   **The Gravest Major Sins (Al-Kaba'ir):** When listing the most grievous of the major sins, the Prophet identified "associating anyone with Allah" as the absolute foremost offense, preceding other severe sins such as disobedience to parents, murder, and false testimony [4, 5]. 
*   **The Seven Noxious Things:** When warning his followers to avoid the seven most destructive acts, the Prophet again listed "associating anything with Allah" as the primary sin, placing it before practicing magic, unjust killing, consuming an orphan's wealth, consuming usury, fleeing the battlefield, and slandering chaste women [6].

gravest in the eye of Allah? He (the Holy Prophet) replied: That you associate a partner with Allah (despite the fact) that He has created you. He (the reporter) said: I told him (the, Holy Prophet): Verily it is indeed grave. He (the reporter) said: I asked him what the next (gravest sin) was. He (the Holy Prophet) replied: That you kill your child out of fear that he shall join you in food. He (the reporter) said: I asked (him) what the next (gravest sin) was. He (the Holy Prophet) observed: Then (the next gravest sin) is that you commit adultery with the wife of your neighbour.
"They will dwell therein forever." (16.29)
